Add the application-defined per-call label described in [gRFC A108](https://github.com/grpc/proposal/blob/master/A108-otel-custom-per-call-label.md) to the following RLS metrics:
* `grpc.lb.rls.default_target_picks`
* `grpc.lb.rls.target_picks`
* `grpc.lb.rls.failed_picks`

author: Pawan Bhardwaj
message:
fix(core): Prevent message bypass on stalled initial metadata (#42823)

Experiment Added: recv_message_filter_bypass_fix

Issues seen and fix:
1. on receiving trailing metadata while initial metadata and message is queued causing filter mutation to bypass.
2. stop upper stack filter resolve trailing metadata promise while the below filter is still processing IM, message, TM. (Added a new queue state for Trailing metadata)
3.  Handle out of band cancellation with the changes with proper return code.

Added test showcasing the scenario.

Closes #42823

PiperOrigin-RevId: 947115571

message:

Remove unused import of `Metadata` from `_base_server.py`. Removing this import had caused some internal test breakage about 2+ years ago and hence had to be re-introduced in https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/37022

Trying to remove it again now to see if this unused import is still needed or safe to remove. Internal tests doesn't show any failures associated with this change now, so I assume this is fine.

author: Mark D. Roth
message:
[xDS] add layer of abstraction to xDS filter chain builder (#42939)

This changes `RouteConfigFilterChainBuilder` to use an xDS-specific filter chain builder API.  This will be needed on the server side, where we need to store the `XdsHttpFilterImpl` pointer instead of the actual C-core filter when we call `RouteConfigFilterChainBuilder`, since we won't be constructing the real filter chain until connection establishment time.

Prior to #42921, this wasn't going to be needed, because the list of filters was always the same for every route, so there was going to be no need to store the actual filter implementations in each individual route.  However, I realized that with the changes in that PR, the list of filters may now be different for each route, so we will actually need to store the filter implementation in each route.

As a nice side-benefit, this approach allowed me to remove the hacky `add_last_filter` parameter to `RouteConfigFilterChainBuilder`.

Closes #42939

COPYBARA_INTEGRATE_REVIEW=https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/42939 from markdroth:xds_routing_builder_indirection e9173b256009972fa435b2e5fe9c4149b64c0bcb
PiperOrigin-RevId: 945856666

message:
fix size_t underflow in alts_tsi_handshaker_result_set_unused_bytes (#42505)

bytes_consumed comes straight from the handshaker response; if it exceeds the recv_bytes length the size_t subtraction underflows and feeds a wild gpr_zalloc/memcpy, so cap it.

author: Rishesh Agarwal
message:
[xDS] Support disabling HTTP filters via LDS/RDS (#42921)

This PR implements the mechanism to support disabling HTTP Connection Manager (HCM) filters at different levels (Virtual Host, Route, Weighted Cluster) via LDS/RDS.

Specifically:

- Adds a disabled field to HttpFilter (LDS) and FilterConfigOverride (RDS) resource structures.
- Parses the disabled field from Envoy FilterConfig protos in LDS/RDS.
- Updates routing logic in XdsRouting to skip disabled filters when building filter chains or generating service configs.
- Adds unit tests in xds_routing_test.cc to verify correct disabling behavior and override priority (HCM < Vhost < Route < ClusterWeight).

Closes #42921

COPYBARA_INTEGRATE_REVIEW=https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/42921 from rishesh007:add_disable_override_config_implementation bc495d561419b758e5039c678ce46350bbba64ef
PiperOrigin-RevId: 945519331

author: Mark D. Roth
message:
[xDS] change ServerConfigSelector to return filter chain (#42478)

Instead of using a channel stack modifier to inject xDS HTTP filters, we now have the `ServerConfigSelector` return the filter chain to use for each RPC.

This rewrites the server config selector filter as an interceptor called `ServerConfigSelectorInterceptor`, which is responsible for injecting the xDS HTTP filters.  The interceptor itself is now automatically added to the filter stack whenever a `ServerConfigSelectorProvider` is present in channel args, which eliminates the need to inject it via the channel stack modifier.  This approach has the nice property that it also adds support for server-side xDS functionality in the v3 stack, which had been previously unsupported, since the channel stack modifier API does not work in the v3 stack.

The filter chains still need to be built for each connection, because they need to be given an `UnstartedCallDestination` that forwards to the next segment of the connection's filter chain.  However, we want to use the same `ServerConfigSelector` object for multiple connections, so that we aren't needlessly duplicating all of the xDS routing state that really needs to exist only once for each L4 filter chain, no matter how many connections use that L4 filter chain.  Therefore, I have added a mechanism whereby the `ServerConfigSelector` can store the filter chains in a separate, per-connection object.

Specifically, I have added a new `ServerConfigSelector::BuildFilterChains()` method that builds the per-connection filter chains.  It returns an object of type `ConnectionState`, which it will use to store the per-connection filter chains.  This connection state object is opaque to the interceptor: it cannot interact with it directly, but it must pass it to `ServerConfigSelector::GetCallConfig()` when determining the call config for each RPC.  The return from `GetCallConfig()` now includes the filter chain to use, and the interceptor will delegate to this filter chain.

For now, the `XdsServerConfigSelector` always uses the same filter chain, regardless of which route is chosen, and it continues to use the existing service config-based mechanism for passing different configs to the filters for each route.  In a subsequent PR, I will change it to instead create a separate filter chain for each route, where the appropriate config is passed to each filter at filter chain build time.

Closes #42478

COPYBARA_INTEGRATE_REVIEW=https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/42478 from markdroth:xds_server_filter_chain_per_route1 081cbf77f776e2b041ad59df8b1019b774091adf
PiperOrigin-RevId: 945299735

author: Mark D. Roth
message:
[telemetry] add plumbing for locality label (roll forward) (#42915)

Rolling forward https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/42803, which was reverted in https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/42870 due to introducing a flake in the outlier detection test.

This PR fixes the flake by changing the test to not change the locality associated with the subchannel when we move it from priority 0 to priority 1.

Closes #42915
